mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or signal loses statistical significance (p>0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, which compresses profit margins and generates negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in >65% of observations, or regime-conditional alpha is not significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate cut below 4.0 percent signals aggressive monetary accommodation that lowers discount rates and stimulates risk appetite, producing positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of instances when FEDFUNDS falls below 4.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with stagnant nonfarm payrolls indicates structural labor market weakness that reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings, driving negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with >50% accuracy, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for macro volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit expectations and equity valuations, generating positive forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a disinflationary environment signals improving supply chain efficiency and margin expansion, triggering positive short-term equity repricing.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during low CPIAUCSL periods yields negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the regime-specific signal loses predictive accuracy.
